heading:
Everyday creative work
shouldn’t be this hard.
lead: Most people don’t want a design studio. They want one correct file, now — without breaking the brand, without waiting on someone, and without handing a private document to a website they’ve never heard of. Lolly exists to take those three frustrations away.

frustrations.title: Off-brand anxiety
frustrations.desc: The quiet dread of picking the wrong colour or font and getting it wrong in public.

frustrations.title: The bottleneck
frustrations.desc: Every small asset stuck in a queue behind a busy designer, a slow tool, or one more approval.

frustrations.title: Cloud dependence
frustrations.desc: Uploading sensitive files just to convert or crop them — and being stranded the moment you’re offline.

matrix.pain: Email a brief to the design team, then wait days for one small graphic.
matrix.relief: Fill in a few fields. The finished asset is ready in seconds.

matrix.pain: Guess at the right font, colour and logo — and hope it passes brand review.
matrix.relief: The design rules are baked into the tool. They bend, not break.

matrix.pain: Upload a contract, payslip or private photo to a random free website to convert or crop it.
matrix.relief: Everything runs on your own device. Nothing is uploaded; nothing leaves the responsible individuals device.

matrix.pain: Re-make the same badge, card or banner a hundred times by hand.
matrix.relief: Paste a spreadsheet. Get a hundred finished, print-ready files at once.

matrix.pain: “Which version is the latest?” — hunting through Slack threads and shared drives.
matrix.relief: The asset is a link. Same inputs, same file, every single time.

matrix.pain: No signal on the plane, or a locked-down network — so you’re simply blocked.
matrix.relief: Works fully offline, on any device, once it has loaded once.

matrix.pain: Embargoed content:  Comms professionals are locked out of engaging collaborators securely.
matrix.relief: lock, encrypt, and password files, zips, and share links - all with zero internet
